metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_5366
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 3: immunogold EM imaging of ArpC2 (aka. Arp2/3) in mouse CA1 hippocampus showed localization to synaptic vesicle membranes and pre/postsynapse (distant from the plasma membrane and release sites).

"ArpC2 was concentrated in dendritic spines approximately 200 nm below the PSD (Figure 3F). However, a fraction of gold particles (~27%) localized to presynaptic terminals with a consistent and specific distribution. ArpC2 was overlapping with synaptic vesicles, but it also localized to the presynaptic area beyond the synaptic vesicle cluster, away from the active zone (mean distance ± SD, 298 ± 159 nm for ArpC2, and 173 ± 129 nm for synaptic vesicles). Very little immunolabeling was observed when the primary antibody was omitted as a negative control. In the few synapses that did have staining (<1%), there was diffuse non-specific signal across the synapse (Figure 3-figure supplement 1D-E). Taken together, the overlapping distributions of Rac1 and Arp2/3 at synaptic vesicles suggest a potential common presynaptic function related to synaptic vesicle modulation.". Experimental description: Results, Figure 3 legend: "Representative pre-embedding immunogold-labeled electron micrographs from 5- to 6-month-old mice in hippocampal CA1 for (C) Rac1 and (D) ArpC2."

Antibody Anti-ArpC2 (rabbit polyclonal) MilliporeSigma Cat# 07-227;
RRID:AB_310447
